2026-04-20T07:08:53.0667006Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-20T07:08:53.0667560Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T07:08:53.0667978Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T07:08:53.0668347Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T07:08:53.0668674Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T07:08:53.0668918Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T07:08:53.0669087Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-20T07:08:53.0669279Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T07:08:53.0669456Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-20T07:08:53.0669942Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-20T07:08:53.0670456Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-20T07:08:53.0670717Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. 2026-04-20T07:11:54.9052787Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T07:10:13.912907901Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":1,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":2434890781,\"number\":45223,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":true,\"title\":\"[BUG] Thread starvation in Azure extensions for DataProtection\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\r\\n\\r\\nAzure.Identity.dll 1.12.0+75fb76... 1.1200.24.31701\\r\\n\\r\\n### Describe the bug\\r\\n\\r\\nWe have Azure Web App service on stage environment based on .net 8 and reproducing thread starvation due to `Azure.Extensions.AspNetCore.DataProtection`.  \\r\\nUnfortunately I can't provide a dump, just because I forgot to do it.\\r\\n\\r\\nWe are using\\r\\n* `Microsoft.AspNetCore.DataProtection` (azure keyvault \\u0026 azure blob storage) for auth cookie.\\r\\n* Azure cients with `ExcludeManagedIdentityCredential\\\": false` and all other credentials are excluded for all azure clients\\r\\n\\r\\n### Expected behavior\\r\\n\\r\\nWorks without thread starvation.\\r\\n\\r\\n### My points:\\r\\n\\r\\n1) Looks like it stuck forever in loading keys from azure storage, possible inside in `AzureBlobXmlRepository`.\\r\\n2) Asp.net should detect long requests and give result, at least log error etc. [Link1](https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/issues/30993) [Link2](https://iditect.com/program-example/c--aspnet-core-middleware-that-cancel-a-request-if-taking-to-long.html)\\r\\n3) Looks like `UseRequestTimeouts` does not help here since it will wait result forever also.\\r\\n4) Asp.net should not process new request if dont have resources. [Link1](https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/issues/45277)\\r\\n\\r\\n### Actual behavior\\r\\n\\r\\n1. Reproducing thread starvation due to `Azure.Extensions.AspNetCore.DataProtection`.  \\r\\n2. Requests hang forever\\r\\n\\r\\n## Related links to this problem.\\r\\n\\r\\n* [Creating two instances of DefaultAzureCredential causes requests using the credential to never complete](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/43796)\\r\\n* [Azure.Extensions.AspNetCore.DataProtection.Keys Heavy load causes blocked threads](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/40174)\\r\\n* [8.0.5 Change - Improve usage of Type.GetType when activating types in data protection](https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/pull/54762)\\r\\n\\r\\n### Reproduction Steps\\r\\n\\r\\n## Code for registering data protection services\\r\\n\\r\\n```cs\\r\\n    public static IServiceCollection AddDataProtectionFromConfiguration(\\r\\n        this IServiceCollection serviceDescriptors_, \\r\\n        IConfiguration configuration_)\\r\\n    {\\r\\n        serviceDescriptors_.Configure\\u003cKeyManagementOptions\\u003e(configuration_.GetRequiredSection(DataProtectionConfigurationSections.KEY_MANAGEMENT));\\r\\n\\r\\n        IDataProtectionBuilder dataProtectionBuilder = serviceDescriptors_.AddDataProtection(configuration_.Bind);\\r\\n\\r\\n        AuthenticatedEncryptorConfiguration encryptorConfiguration = new();\\r\\n        configuration_.GetRequiredSection(DataProtectionConfigurationSections.KEY_MANAGEMENT_ALGORITHMS).Bind(encryptorConfiguration);\\r\\n        dataProtectionBuilder.UseCryptographicAlgorithms(encryptorConfiguration);\\r\\n\\r\\n        var keyVaultOptions = configuration_.GetRequiredSection(DataProtectionConfigurationSections.KEY_VAULT).Get\\u003cKeyVaultDataProtectionOptions\\u003e()!;\\r\\n        if (keyVaultOptions.Enabled)\\r\\n        {\\r\\n            var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential(keyVaultOptions.Credential);\\r\\n            dataProtectionBuilder.ProtectKeysWithAzureKeyVault(new Uri(keyVaultOptions.KeyIdentifier), credential);\\r\\n        }\\r\\n\\r\\n        var blobsConfiguration = configuration_.GetRequiredSection(DataProtectionConfigurationSections.BLOB_STORAGE);\\r\\n        var blobStorageOptions = blobsConfiguration.Get\\u003cBlobStorageOptions\\u003e()!;\\r\\n        if (blobStorageOptions.Enabled)\\r\\n        {\\r\\n            serviceDescriptors_.AddAzureClients(clientBuilder =\\u003e\\r\\n            {\\r\\n                var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential(blobStorageOptions.Credential);\\r\\n                clientBuilder\\r\\n                    .AddBlobServiceClient(blobsConfiguration.GetRequiredSection(DataProtectionConfigurationSections.AZURE_CONNECTION_SUBSECTION))\\r\\n                    .WithCredential(credential)\\r\\n                    ;\\r\\n            });\\r\\n\\r\\n            dataProtectionBuilder.PersistKeysToAzureBlobStorage((serviceProvider) =\\u003e\\r\\n            {\\r\\n                return serviceProvider\\r\\n                    .GetRequiredService\\u003cBlobServiceClient\\u003e()\\r\\n                    .GetBlobContainerClient(blobStorageOpftions.ContainerName)\\r\\n                    .GetBlobClient(blobStorageOptions.BlobName);\\r\\n            });\\r\\n        }\\r\\n\\r\\n        return serviceDescriptors_;\\r\\n    }\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\n## Logs\\r\\n\\r\\nWe can provide more Because their build configuration treats NU190x audit warnings as errors, the build is blocked. They are requesting a timeline to upgrade the transitive dependency.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📋 Issue Details\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n**Package:** `Azure.Extensions.AspNetCore.DataProtection.Keys 1.6.1` and `Azure.Extensions.AspNetCore.DataProtection.Blobs 1.5.1`\n**Affected API:** Transitive dependency on `System.Security.Cryptography.Xml`\n**Scenarios:**\n- NuGet vulnerability audit warnings (NU190x) treated as errors causing restore/build failures\n- Transitive dependency chain introduces known CVEs via GHSA-37gx-xxp4-5rgx and GHSA-w3x6-4m5h-cxqf\n\n**Root ask:** A timeline for upgrading `System.Security.Cryptography.Xml` to a patched version in the DataProtection packages\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe vulnerability is transitive — neither of the Azure packages directly exposes the vulnerable API, but the NuGet audit surfacing it as an error blocks the customer's build. The referenced dotnet/aspnetcore issue (#66348) suggests this is a known concern in the ecosystem.\n\n**Suggested investigation steps:**\n1. Confirm the exact `System.Security.Cryptography.Xml` version pinned by each DataProtection package and whether newer versions resolve the advisories\n2. Check if a dependency update in the affected packages can be released (either patch or minor bump) to pull in a fixed version\n3.
